name: Valdespartera-Plaza Trail
name_original: Parque Lineal de Plaza — circular junto al Canal Imperial de Aragón
description: This easy 3.0-mile jogging route through Parque Lineal de Plaza follows the Canal Imperial de Aragón, featuring paved paths and the Solar La
region_intro: Jogging around Zaragoza offers diverse landscapes, from urban green spaces to riverine environments and natural reserves. The city is characterized by the Ebro River and its tributaries, which provide green corridors and scenic routes. Protected areas like the Galacho de Juslibol feature tranquil paths through lagoons and gypsum escarpments, while city parks such as José Antonio Labordeta Park and Parque del Agua offer flat running paths. The region also includes more challenging trails in areas with varied elevation. Best jogging routes around Zaragoza The most popular jogging route is Views of the Basilica of Our Lady of the Pilar — circular along the Ebro River, a 2.9 miles (4.6 km) trail that takes about 29 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of city landmarks along the Ebro River. Another top favourite among local runners is Valdespartera-Plaza Trail, an easy 3.0 miles (4.8 km) path. This route provides a straightforward urban running experience. Local runners also love the San Caprasio Circular Trail from Farlete, a 10.6 miles (17.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 12 minutes. Jogging around Zaragoza is defined by the Ebro River, urban parks, and natural reserves, providing a range of experiences from flat riverside paths to more challenging trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy jogging routes to more difficult running trails. The routes in Zaragoza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 14,000 runners have used komoot to explore Zaragoza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Zaragoza?
A: There are nearly 500 running routes around Zaragoza, offering a wide variety of options for all fitness levels. You can find everything from easy urban jogs to challenging mountain trails.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on Zaragoza's running trails?
A: Zaragoza offers a diverse range of terrains for runners. You'll find flat, scenic paths along the Ebro River and its tributaries, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983629485/views-of-the-basilica-of-our-lady-of-the-pilar-circular-along-the-ebro-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Basilica of Our Lady of the Pilar — circular along the Ebro River&lt;/a&gt;. There are also green corridors in urban parks like José Antonio Labordeta Park and Parque del Agua, and more challenging, hilly trails in areas like Moncayo Natural Park or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983261477/san-caprasio-circular-trail-from-farlete" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Caprasio Circular Trail from Farlete&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or casual joggers?
A: Yes, Zaragoza has many easy running routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983609254/valdespartera-plaza-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdespartera-Plaza Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a straightforward urban path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983306812/galachos-de-juslibol-and-miranda-castle-via-the-ebro-natural-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galachos de Juslibol and Miranda Castle via the Ebro Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt; offers a peaceful, flat experience through a natural reserve. The towpaths along the Canal Imperial de Aragón also provide excellent, relatively flat options.

Q: Where can I find challenging running trails with significant elevation in Zaragoza?
A: For more challenging runs, consider routes that venture into the more rugged terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983275808/maria-de-huerva-castle-and-cabezo-sillon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;María de Huerva Castle and Cabezo Sillón Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 21.5 km trail with substantial elevation gain. Moncayo Natural Park, though further afield, also offers demanding trails through forests and mountainous landscapes for experienced runners.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or natural features to see while running in Zaragoza?
A: Many routes offer scenic views and points of interest. Along the Ebro River, you can see the Basilica of Our Lady of the Pilar. The Galacho de Juslibol natural reserve features lagoons and gypsum escarpments. For panoramic views, consider routes that pass by viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/94970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de las Planas&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6575360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de las Zorras&lt;/a&gt;. Some trails also lead to historical sites, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983238854/running-to-the-roman-dam-of-almonacid-de-la-cuba-and-poza-de-los-chorros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Dam of Almonacid de la Cuba&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Zaragoza area?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983629485/views-of-the-basilica-of-our-lady-of-the-pilar-circular-along-the-ebro-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Basilica of Our Lady of the Pilar — circular along the Ebro River&lt;/a&gt; is a popular easy loop. Another option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983620625/circular-del-vedado-de-penaflor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular del Vedado de Peñaflor&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a longer circular experience.

Q: What do other runners think about the trails in Zaragoza?
A: The running routes in Zaragoza are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 120 reviews. More than 14,000 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic riverside paths and the accessibility of urban green spaces.

Q: Are there any running routes that are particularly suitable for families?
A: For family-friendly running, consider the flat and well-maintained paths in urban parks like José Antonio Labordeta Park (Parque Grande) or Parque del Agua Luis Buñuel. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983306812/galachos-de-juslibol-and-miranda-castle-via-the-ebro-natural-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galachos de Juslibol and Miranda Castle via the Ebro Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt; is also a great option, offering a peaceful natural setting that can be accessed by a small train from the city.

Q: Can I find long-distance running trails in Zaragoza?
A: Yes, Zaragoza offers several long-distance options for endurance runners. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983275808/maria-de-huerva-castle-and-cabezo-sillon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;María de Huerva Castle and Cabezo Sillón Loop&lt;/a&gt; is over 21 km, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983251798/monegrillo-viewpoints-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monegrillo Viewpoints Trail&lt;/a&gt; covers over 20 km, both providing extensive running experiences. Moncayo Natural Park also features the GR 90 long-distance path for those seeking even greater distances.

Q: Are there any running routes near lakes or water bodies in Zaragoza?
A: Many running routes follow the Ebro River and its tributaries, offering pleasant waterside views. For specific lakes, you might find routes near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1371792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Lake&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5238060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balsa de Larralde&lt;/a&gt;. The Galacho de Juslibol also features tranquil lagoons along its paths.

Q: What are the best times of year for running in Zaragoza?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for running in Zaragoza, with mild temperatures. Summer can be quite hot, especially midday, so early mornings or late evenings are preferable. Winter is usually mild, making it suitable for running, though some higher elevation trails might experience colder conditions.

Q: Are there any routes that offer a mix of urban and natural scenery?
A: Absolutely. Many routes along the Ebro River, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983629485/views-of-the-basilica-of-our-lady-of-the-pilar-circular-along-the-ebro-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Basilica of Our Lady of the Pilar — circular along the Ebro River&lt;/a&gt;, blend urban landmarks with natural riverine environments. The Parque del Agua Luis Buñuel also offers flat paths that lead to the Ebro's edge, providing a balance of parkland and river views.
tips: Inside the Parque Lineal de Plaza we find this labyrinth. It is the Solar labyrinth, longer than the Baroque labyrinth that we have at the other end of the park, so if we want to do it we will have to add half an hour to our activity but it is surely worth doing, especially if we go with the More smalls.

A beautiful area of the Parque Lineal de Plaza where different paths will lead us to this large lake inhabited by various species of birds such as ducks or swans. Ideal for playing sports or resting and breathing nature.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Valdespartera-Plaza Trail is classified as an easy jogging route, making it ideal for beginners and all fitness levels. The terrain is mostly paved and generally flat, offering a comfortable experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features paved surfaces, which makes for an easy and smooth jogging experience. It follows the pleasant natural environment of the Canal Imperial de Aragón, with continuous tree cover.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: As you jog, you'll pass by interesting features such as the Solar Labyrinth, an artistic intervention within the park. You'll also enjoy views of the Plaza Lake and the lush vegetation bordering the Canal Imperial de Aragón.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Valdespartera-Plaza Trail is part of a larger network. This route covers 100% of the Valdespartera-Plaza trail, and also passes through sections of the Valdefierro-Plaza (58%) and the Plaza de las cinco Culturas (20%) trails.

faqs.question: Is the Valdespartera-Plaza Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, urban parks in Zaragoza, including the Parque Lineal de Plaza where this trail is located, are dog-friendly. However, dogs must be kept on a leash at all times, and owners are expected to clean up after their pets. Always check for specific signage within the park for any updated regulations.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for jogging. In summer, early mornings or late evenings are best to avoid the heat, while winter jogging is comfortable on the paved surfaces, though you might want to layer up.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Parque Lineal de Plaza?
faqs.answer: No, there are no entrance fees or special permits required to access the Parque Lineal de Plaza or to jog this trail. It is a public green space freely accessible to everyone.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: Since the trail is located within the Parque Lineal de Plaza, you can look for street parking in the residential areas adjacent to the park. Specific parking lots are not typically designated for the linear park itself, but ample street parking can usually be found nearby.

faqs.question: How can I get to the trail by public transport?
faqs.answer: The Parque Lineal de Plaza is well-connected by Zaragoza's public transport network. Various bus lines serve the areas surrounding the park. Check local bus routes that pass near the Canal Imperial de Aragón or the Valdespartera neighborhood for the most convenient access points.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Given its easy difficulty, paved surfaces, and the pleasant environment of the Parque Lineal de Plaza, this trail is very suitable for families with children. It's a great spot for a leisurely jog or walk together.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restrooms nearby?
faqs.answer: While the Parque Lineal de Plaza itself is primarily a green space, you'll find various amenities in the surrounding urban areas. Depending on your starting point, there are cafes, shops, and public restrooms available in the nearby neighborhoods of Valdespartera and other areas bordering the park.
